steps.title: Separate the opening clause
steps.content: The sentence begins with a dependent clause introduced by "While": the greater adjutant can be found in places including Pakistan. The next part, "more than 80 percent of this endangered stork species is found in Assam, India," is the main clause.

steps.title: Use a comma after the clause
steps.content: B uses a semicolon and C uses a period, but the opening dependent clause should not be split into a separate sentence. D omits the needed boundary. A comma correctly separates the introductory dependent clause from the main clause. The answer is A.
